Bay.title: Bay
Bay.description: Represents a physical bay or service station within the SparkFlow car wash facility, used for managing vehicle occupancy.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Bay entity.

name.description: Human-readable name of the bay (e.g., 'Bay 1', 'Detailing Bay').

status.description: Current operational status of the bay (e.g., 'Available', 'Occupied', 'Under Maintenance').

Service.title: Service
Service.description: Defines the various services offered by SparkFlow, including pricing and estimated duration.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Service entity.

name.description: The name of the service (e.g., 'Deluxe Wash', 'Interior Vacuum').

basePrice.description: The standard base price for the service.

estimatedDurationMinutes.description: The estimated time in minutes required to complete the service.

category.description: The category of the service (e.g., 'Wash', 'Detailing', 'Tinting', 'Carpet Cleaning').

Staff.title: Staff
Staff.description: Manages information about SparkFlow employees, including their roles, pay structures, and performance metrics.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Staff entity.

name.description: The full name of the staff member.

role.description: The role of the staff member within SparkFlow (e.g., 'Agent', 'Attendant', 'Manager').

payType.description: The method of payment for the staff member (e.g., 'Daily', 'Monthly', 'Commission').

performanceRating.description: An aggregated rating of the staff member's performance.

totalEarned.description: The total amount earned by the staff member over a period, useful for payroll calculations.

phoneNumber.description: The contact phone number of the staff member.

email.description: The email address of the staff member.

InventoryItem.title: InventoryItem
InventoryItem.description: Records details of consumable items and products used or sold by SparkFlow.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InventoryItem entity.

name.description: The name of the inventory item (e.g., 'Car Soap', 'Microfiber Towel').

stockCount.description: The current quantity of the item in stock.

wholesalePrice.description: The price at which SparkFlow purchases the item from suppliers.

retailPrice.description: The price at which SparkFlow sells the item to customers, if applicable.

Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Stores customer profiles for CRM purposes, including contact details, visit history, and reward points for SparkFlow's gamification features.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Customer entity.

name.description: The full name of the customer.

phoneNumber.description: The primary contact phone number for the customer, used for SMS broadcasts.

email.description: The email address of the customer.

registrationDate.description: The date and time when the customer was registered in the SparkFlow system.

lastVisit.description: The date and time of the customer's most recent visit to SparkFlow.

totalRewardPoints.description: The cumulative reward points earned by the customer through SparkFlow's gamification program.

CustomerBadge.title: CustomerBadge
CustomerBadge.description: Tracks badges awarded to customers as part of SparkFlow's gamification system, linked to specific achievements.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CustomerBadge entity.

customerId.description: Reference to the customer who earned this badge. (Relationship: Customer 1:N CustomerBadge)

badgeName.description: The name of the badge awarded (e.g., 'Wash Streak Master', 'First Timer').

description.description: A brief description of the badge and how it was earned.

awardedDate.description: The date and time when the badge was awarded to the customer.

streakCount.description: Relevant count for badges related to streaks (e.g., number of consecutive visits).

Vehicle.title: Vehicle
Vehicle.description: Represents a vehicle currently being managed by the SparkFlow system, from check-in to exit, including its status and selected services.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Vehicle entity (typically the plate number of the vehicle).

customerId.description: Reference to the customer who owns or brought the vehicle. (Relationship: Customer 1:N Vehicle)

status.description: Current status of the vehicle in the SparkFlow workflow (e.g., 'Queue', 'In-Bay', 'Ready for Payment', 'Completed').

arrivalTime.description: Timestamp when the vehicle arrived and was checked into the SparkFlow system.

bayId.description: Reference to the bay where the vehicle is currently located, if applicable. (Relationship: Bay 1:N Vehicle)

assignedAttendantId.description: Reference to the staff attendant assigned to service this vehicle. (Relationship: Staff 1:N Vehicle)

selectedServiceIds.description: References to the services selected for this vehicle during check-in. (Relationship: Service N:N Vehicle)

exitTime.description: Timestamp when the vehicle exited the SparkFlow system after service completion and payment.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Records details of financial transactions for services rendered by SparkFlow, including payment status and M-Pesa integration details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity (order ID).

vehicleId.description: Reference to the vehicle associated with this transaction. (Relationship: Vehicle 1:N Transaction)

serviceIds.description: References to the specific services included in this transaction. (Relationship: Service N:N Transaction)

amount.description: The total amount charged for the services in this transaction.

paymentStatus.description: The current payment status of the transaction (e.g., 'Pending', 'Paid', 'Failed').

transactionTime.description: The date and time when the transaction was initiated or completed.

mpesaReceiptNumber.description: The M-Pesa receipt number for successful payments made via M-Pesa.

durationMinutes.description: The calculated duration in minutes for the service completion, from arrival to exit.

LogisticsRequest.title: LogisticsRequest
LogisticsRequest.description: Manages requests for pickup and delivery services, such as home carpet cleaning, including location and delivery status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LogisticsRequest entity.

customerId.description: Reference to the customer who made the logistics request. (Relationship: Customer 1:N LogisticsRequest)

pickupAddress.description: The physical address for picking up the item.

itemType.description: The type of item being handled for logistics (e.g., 'Home Carpet', 'Furniture').

gpsLocation.description: The GPS coordinates of the pickup/delivery location (e.g., 'latitude,longitude').

deliveryStatus.description: The current status of the logistics request (e.g., 'Pending Pickup', 'In Transit', 'Delivered').

eta.description: The estimated time of arrival for pickup or delivery.

requestTime.description: The date and time when the logistics request was made.

assignedStaffId.description: Reference to the staff member assigned to handle this logistics request. (Relationship: Staff 1:N LogisticsRequest)

definition.description: Operational status of car wash bays. Accessible by managers and system processes.

params.name: bayId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific car wash bay.

definition.description: Defines car wash services, pricing, and duration. Publicly readable for display, writeable by managers.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific car wash service.

definition.description: Staff profiles with roles and performance metrics. Staff can read their own, managers can read/write all. `staffId` matches Firebase Auth UID.

params.name: staffId
params.description: Unique identifier for a staff member, typically matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Records of consumable inventory items. Accessible by managers for stock management.

params.name: inventoryItemId
params.description: Unique identifier for an inventory item.

definition.description: Customer profiles for CRM. Customers can read/update their own. Agents can create. Managers can read/write all. `customerId` typically matches Firebase Auth UID for authenticated customers. Includes denormalized `lastVisit` and `totalRewardPoints`.

params.name: customerId
params.description: Unique identifier for a customer, typically matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Badges awarded to a specific customer as part of gamification. Customers can read their own badges. System (Cloud Function) writes. Includes denormalized `customerId` for authorization independence.

params.name: customerId
params.description: Unique identifier of the customer who owns the badge.

params.name: customerBadgeId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific badge awarded to the customer.

definition.description: Real-time status of vehicles in the car wash workflow. Managers/Attendants can read/write. Customers can read their associated vehicle's status. Includes denormalized `customerId` and `assignedAttendantId` for authorization independence and QAPs.

params.name: vehicleId
params.description: Unique identifier for a vehicle (e.g., license plate number).

definition.description: Financial transaction records. Managers read/write all. Customers read their own. Includes denormalized `customerId` (from the associated vehicle) for authorization independence and QAPs.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: Unique identifier for a financial transaction or order.

definition.description: Requests for pickup/delivery logistics (e.g., home carpet cleaning). Customers can create/read their own. Managers can read/write all. Assigned staff can read/update their assigned requests. Includes denormalized `customerId` and `assignedStaffId` for authorization independence and QAPs.

params.name: logisticsRequestId
params.description: Unique identifier for a logistics request.

definition.description: Collection to grant 'Agent' role. Document existence signifies role. `agentId` matches Firebase Auth UID. Accessible by managers for assignment.

params.name: agentId
params.description: The UID of a staff member who has the 'Agent' role.

definition.description: Collection to grant 'Attendant' role. Document existence signifies role. `attendantId` matches Firebase Auth UID. Accessible by managers for assignment.

params.name: attendantId
params.description: The UID of a staff member who has the 'Attendant' role.

definition.description: Collection to grant 'Manager' role. Document existence signifies role. `managerId` matches Firebase Auth UID. Accessible by managers for assignment.

params.name: managerId
params.description: The UID of a staff member who has the 'Manager' role.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for 'SparkFlow Ops' is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and clear Access Modeling, adhering to the core design principles of security, scalability, and debuggability. All entities are housed in top-level collections or direct subcollections, with explicit denormalization to ensure efficient and secure authorization.

**Authorization Independence:** This is achieved by embedding critical authorization context directly within documents where access might otherwise require traversing parent documents. For instance:
*   `vehicles_live` documents include `customerId` and `assignedAttendantId`. This allows customers to directly query and access their vehicle's status (using `customerId == request.auth.uid`) and attendants to manage their assigned jobs (using `assignedAttendantId == request.auth.uid`) without needing to perform `get()` operations on `/customers/{customerId}` or `/staff/{attendantId}` from within security rules.
*   `transactions` documents similarly include `customerId` to enable customers to view their past transactions securely and directly.
*   `logistics_requests` documents contain both `customerId` and `assignedStaffId`, facilitating independent authorization checks for both customers and assigned staff.
*   `customer_badges` (a subcollection of `customers`) naturally inherits `customerId` from its path, and the document itself includes `customerId` for redundant verification, ensuring secure access without complex lookups.

**QAPs (Secure List Operations):** The structure facilitates secure `list` operations by ensuring that collections either have a homogeneous security posture (e.g., `services` are publicly readable, `inventory_items` are manager-only) or by denormalizing ownership fields. This allows Firestore Security Rules to leverage `request.auth.uid` against document fields (`resource.data.customerId` or `resource.data.assignedAttendantId`) in `list` operations, effectively filtering results at the rule level without allowing arbitrary access or requiring client-side filtering of sensitive data. For role-based access, dedicated `/roles_*` collections are used, allowing rules to check for document existence (`exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_managers/$(request.auth.uid))`) for manager-level `list` permissions. This design ensures that all `list` operations are implicitly secure and do not act as filters for already-retrieved data.

**Structural Segregation:** Collections like `services`, `bays`, and `inventory_items` contain documents with uniform access requirements. Customer-specific data (e.g., `customers`, `customer_badges`) is organized hierarchically where appropriate, and access is strictly controlled by `request.auth.uid` matching the path or denormalized `ownerId`/`customerId` fields. Role-based access is managed through separate, top-level `roles_agents`, `roles_attendants`, and `roles_managers` collections, simplifying rule logic by segregating access control data.

This design, branded 'SparkFlow Ops', prioritizes robust security rules that are easy to write, understand, and debug, while maintaining performance for real-time syncing across various user interfaces including the staff tablets and customer web portal.
